text.title: fine-tuning-openai-data-prep Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for fine-tuning-openai-data-prep as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Uploading training data without format validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Malformed JSONL fails only at job start, not at upload. A job that runs for 30 minutes before failing on a malformed line wastes API time and budget. Always validate locally before uploading.

failure-mode.description: Including PII in training data without a Data Processing Agreement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OpenAI processes training data on their infrastructure. Uploading data containing personal information (names, emails, health records) without a DPA and explicit user consent violates GDPR and may violate the OpenAI Terms of Service. Scrub PII before uploading.

failure-mode.description: Using inconsistent system prompts across training examples.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inconsistent system prompts teach the model conflicting behavior baselines. The fine-tuned model learns to respond differently based on minor system prompt variations, producing unpredictable production behavior. Use identical wording for all examples.
